title: DeerFlow file upload crashes due to environment variable resolution error
description:
## Problem
DeerFlow gateway crashes when attempting to upload files through the web interface at http://localhost:2026.

## Error
```python
File "/app/backend/packages/harness/deerflow/config/app_config.py", line 287, in resolve_env_variables
    return {k: cls.resolve_env_variables(v) for k, v in config.items()}
```

The error occurs during environment variable resolution in the config loading process, triggered by the file upload endpoint.

## Impact
- File upload feature completely broken
- Users cannot attach files to DeerFlow tasks
- Workaround: paste content inline or use file paths in mounted directories

## Reproduction Steps
1. Navigate to http://localhost:2026
2. Start a new chat/task
3. Attempt to upload any file using the upload button
4. Gateway crashes with config resolution error

## Root Cause
Environment variable resolution in `app_config.py` fails during upload operation. Likely causes:
- Undefined environment variable referenced in config.yaml
- Circular reference in config variable resolution
- Type mismatch in environment variable substitution

## Investigation Context
Discovered during attempt to upload comprehensive audit prompt file. Full investigation documented in `docs/ops/deerflow-file-access-investigation.md`.

## Affected Files
- `deployment/deer-flow/backend/packages/harness/deerflow/config/app_config.py` (line 287)
- `deployment/deer-flow/backend/app/gateway/routers/uploads.py` (line 254)
- `deployment/deer-flow/config.yaml` (environment variable references)

## Recommended Fix
1. Add error handling to `resolve_env_variables()` to identify which variable is failing
2. Validate all environment variables referenced in config.yaml are defined in .env
3. Add unit tests for config loading with missing/invalid env vars
4. Consider lazy loading config only when needed, not on every upload request

## Workaround
Users can work around by:
- Pasting file content directly into chat
- Referencing files by path in mounted directories (/mnt/host/aisci/)
- Using API with pre-uploaded files to /mnt/user-data/uploads/

## Priority Justification
HIGH - Core feature completely broken, affects all users attempting file uploads. However, workarounds exist and file access via mounted paths works correctly.
